summary.intent: Fix six bugs identified in a second Codex review pass for a calendar UI component in a Rust/ars-ui project, following a test-driven approach with spec synchronization.
summary.outcome: All six bugs were fixed with 9 new regression tests added (130→139 passing tests), no snapshot churn, clean clippy, healthy coverage, and the spec synced before pushing.

repo: Calendar component follows a state-machine pattern (Machine/Event/Context) mirroring date_field: SyncProps event + on_props_changed hook is the established pattern for parent controlled-prop propagation.
spec/components/date-time/calendar.md is kept in sync with implementation and validated as part of the pre-push gate.
xci-fast is the pre-push gate command; passing it is required before commit+push.
Snapshots use root_attrs, not trigger attrs, so adding HTML disabled to nav buttons (N4 fix) did not cause snapshot churn.
weeks_for contract is always 6 rows; returning partial rows on boundary failure violates the spec — return empty instead.

code.finding: Initial focused_date must be clamped to min/max; value/default_value/today are valid sources but unclamped values cause the cell to get both tabindex=0 and HTML disabled with no valid focus target.
set_props must dispatch SyncProps event and handle it via on_props_changed hook to propagate parent controlled-prop changes — same pattern as date_field.
SetMonth/SetYear arms only updated visible_month/year, leaving focused_date stale; they must also advance focused_date in sync.
is_prev_disabled/is_next_disabled only checked min/max bounds; ctx.disabled must also disable both nav triggers.
month_step_plan must be atomic: attempt focused_date arithmetic first and abort the entire plan (including visible_month advance) if it fails.
weeks_for must return empty Vec on add_days failure near calendar boundaries rather than returning a partial week, to uphold the always-6-rows contract.

workflow: Write failing tests for all findings before implementing any fixes — confirms the test correctly captures the bug before fixing it.
Verify 'expected failures' count matches finding count before proceeding to implementation.
After implementation, check for snapshot churn explicitly to confirm no unintended rendering regressions.
Spec sync is part of the standard commit checklist alongside clippy and coverage.
Codex review passes are iterative; second-pass findings (N1–N6) were distinct from first-pass findings.

friction: Clippy caught an issue after implementation requiring an additional edit+retry cycle before the pre-push gate could run.
Multiple spec sections required individual targeted edits (§1.2, §1.6, §1.7, §1.8) with no bulk-update mechanism, making spec sync tedious.
Finding anchor locations in mod.rs required repeated grep/Read calls due to file size.
open_items: The SyncProps bypass-guard in the transition function and on_props_changed hook should be verified against the date_field implementation to ensure full parity.
cell_trigger spec block was noted as still having an old version mid-session and was patched — worth a final diff review to confirm the spec fully reflects T1 semantics.
No snapshot was updated for N4 (nav button disabled state) because existing snapshots use root_attrs; a dedicated snapshot covering trigger disabled state would improve regression coverage.
